definitions.en: An expression of surprise or realization.
definitions.es: Una expresión de sorpresa o realización.
definitions.vi: Một biểu hiện của sự ngạc nhiên hoặc nhận ra.

content.sentence: Ồ, tôi vừa thấy ___ điều thú vị.

translation.en: Oh, I just saw an interesting thing.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Oh, I didn't know you came so early today.
Oh, I just received good news from my family.
Oh, I thought we didn't have to go to school today.
exampleSentences: Ồ, anh không biết hôm nay em đến sớm như vậy đâu.
Ồ, chị vừa mới nhận được tin vui từ gia đình.
Ồ, em tưởng hôm nay chúng ta không phải đi học.
notes:
Ồ = “oh.” It shows surprise, sudden realization, or noticing something.

It’s an interjection, usually at the beginning of a sentence, followed by a comma in writing. You say it when you just understood something or didn’t expect it.

Softer and more neutral than ôi (which sounds more emotional or dramatic). Sometimes close to à when you suddenly get it, but à feels more like “ah, I see,” while ồ feels more like “oh, really?”
notes_md:
**Ồ** = “oh.” It shows surprise, sudden realization, or noticing something.

It’s an interjection, usually at the beginning of a sentence, followed by a comma in writing. You say it when you just understood something or didn’t expect it.

Softer and more neutral than **ôi** (which sounds more emotional or dramatic). Sometimes close to **à** when you suddenly get it, but **à** feels more like “ah, I see,” while **ồ** feels more like “oh, really?”

translation.en: Oh, I just saw an interesting thing.

content.sentence: Ồ, tôi vừa thấy ___ điều thú vị.
